title: Pre-flight canonical leak audit

protocol.feature_columns: cols 512-575 (window slot 8 = last input check-in's per-visit embedding)

verdict: PASS — canonical leak-probe floor reproduces historical AL value within σ (31.04 vs 30.84 ± 2.02). FL/AZ first-time measurements pinned. Every later Tier-1+ experiment MUST report its leak-probe value; red-flag threshold = +5 pp vs canonical (per INDEX.html T1.1 success criteria).
notes: The historical script scripts/probe/leak_sniff_ijm.py uses embeddings.parquet (per-check-in rows) — that protocol returns ~96% macro-F1 trivially because the check2hgi r2c boundary trains the embedding to encode its own POI category. The protocol that matches the historical 30.84% floor is the next.parquet last-window probe documented here.

Floor is the gate for sequence-edge leak. Any later c2hgi variant that lifts this number by >5 pp at any state has likely introduced an unexpected leak path and should be audited before being used in subsequent tiers.
